name: Re-enter The PTX Hot-Band Path With A Mid-Width Grouping Control

summary.name: Re-enter The PTX Hot-Band Path With A Mid-Width Grouping Control
summary.hypothesis: The regression is dominated by falling off the PTX hot-band microkernel and its single-stage export-scratch path, not by the narrower grouped-row value alone. Restoring the PTX launch/store path while testing a bounded middle grouping such as 6 rows should keep DRAM near the 25.643007 ms active baseline and let the grouped-row idea be measured on the intended PTX baseline instead of on the higher-traffic non-PTX sibling.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Long-scoreboard in the PTX hot-band kernel, with DRAM and L2 locality acting as guardrails while the grouped-row mapping is re-isolated.
summary.risk: Low to medium. This is close to the best active family and directly addresses the confounding variable, but the upside may be small if grouped-row width was never the real limiter once the PTX path is restored.
summary.stop_condition: Close this line if the PTX microkernel is restored, hot-band DRAM returns near the active-baseline level, and the runtime still does not beat or meaningfully challenge 25.64300728 ms.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
